data_in.description: Peak-picked feature table in HDF5 format (deimos-formatted) containing m/z, drift_time, retention_time, and intensity columns

data_out.description: Annotated feature table (HDF5) with isotopologue labels including monoisotopic designation, isotope membership count, and C13 mass offsets

domain_knowledge: C13 natural isotope abundance results in discrete mass offsets of approximately 1.003355 Da per substitution, detectable in high-resolution m/z measurements.
Singly charged monoisotopic species (z=+1) exhibit isotope patterns where the M+1, M+2, etc. peaks correspond to successive C13 substitutions in the molecular formula.
Isotopic signature quality assessment requires minimum cluster membership (≥3 peaks) to distinguish true isotope patterns from noise or accidental peak co-elution.
Drift time and retention time dimensions in the feature table enable spatial coherence filtering to confirm that isotopologues co-localize in both ion mobility and chromatographic space.

direct_checks: verify that input file exists and is readable HDF5 format
verify that deimos.isotopes function executes without error on the peak-picked feature table
verify that output table file exists and is in expected format (CSV or HDF5)
verify that output table contains at least one row of annotated isotopologue data
verify that output table contains required columns for isotope annotation (e.g., isotope_offset, parent_mz, charge_state)
verify that all detected isotope offsets are consistent with C13 mass difference (~1.003 Da) for singly charged species

verify that output matches structure described in deimos isotope detection documentation — no canonical answer for exact column names across versions, parameter-sensitive to input peak-picking thresholds
expert_review: assess whether detected C13 isotopologue offsets are chemically reasonable and match expected isotope patterns for biological small molecules
evaluate whether the isotope annotation quality and false-discovery rate are consistent with published deimos validation results
review whether singly charged species assumption is appropriately applied and documented in output labelling

[intro] Annotated feature table (HDF5) with isotopologue labels including monoisotopic designation, isotope membership count, and C13 mass offsets: 'characterized by mass, CCS, tandem mass spectra, and isotopic signature'
[intro] Python: 'is a Python application programming interface and command-line tool'
[discussion] No changelog or version history available for deimos package: '_No changelog found._'

cmd: import deimos; import pandas as pd; import h5py; peaks = pd.read_hdf('{inputs.peaks_h5}'); isotopes = deimos.isotopes.detect(peaks, charge=1, min_cluster_size=3); isotopes.to_hdf('{inputs.peaks_h5}'.replace('.h5', '_annotated.h5'), key='features', mode='w')
executable.grounding_notes: Unverified by the executable guard — command runs inline code (-c/-e); its API calls cannot be validated against package files. Treat this command as a best-effort inference; verify the entry point and APIs against the real code before running.

expected_outputs: Annotated feature table (HDF5) with isotopologue labels including monoisotopic designation, isotope membership count, and C13 mass offsets
finding: DEIMoS isotope detection enumerates C13 isotopologue offsets by specifying an m/z delta of 1.003355 Da with up to 5 isotopic substitutions for singly charged species, using m/z tolerance, drift time tolerance, and retention time tolerance to constrain the search before applying a maximum m/z error of 50 ppm for final downselection.
inputs: Peak-picked feature table in HDF5 format (deimos-formatted) containing m/z, drift_time, retention_time, and intensity columns
methodology_summary: Load peak-picked HDF5 feature table with m/z, drift_time, retention_time, and intensity columns.
Invoke deimos.isotopes module to enumerate C13 isotopologue mass offsets for charge +1 species by computing pairwise m/z differences and spatial concordance.
Filter detected isotopic signatures to retain clusters with minimum 3 members.
Annotate feature table rows with isotopologue identity labels (monoisotopic flag, member index, C13 count) and export to HDF5.

Validation: Confirm output HDF5 file contains isotope annotation columns; verify all detected isotopic signatures satisfy ≥3-member threshold and mass offset consistency with theoretical C13 shift (1.003355 Da per substitution).

task_description: Apply DEIMoS isotope detection to a peak-picked HDF5 feature table to identify C13 isotopologue offsets for singly charged species and produce an annotated output table labeling detected isotopologues.

task_inputs.label: Peak-picked feature table in HDF5 format

task_objective: Apply deimos.isotopes.detect() to a peak-picked feature table to identify C13 isotopologue clusters and annotate detected isotopologues with charge state, isotope count, and m/z error in an output dataframe.

task_outputs.label: Annotated feature table with isotopologue labels

title: Reconstruct Feature Alignment Across Two Sample Datasets Using Reference-based Alignment
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Load peak-picked feature table from HDF5 file using deimos.load() with columns for m/z, drift_time, retention_time, and intensity. 2. Apply deimos.isotopes to detect C13 isotopologue patterns, computing mass offsets between monoisotopic and isotopically substituted peaks for charge state +1. 3. Filter detected isotopic signatures to retain only those with at least 3 members using a membership threshold criterion. 4. Annotate the feature table with isotopologue labels (monoisotopic designation, isotope count, offset mass) and export as HDF5 with isotope metadata columns.
